page.title: Board
page.subtitle: Three parallel plans, separate targets and satisfaction

plans.title: Three plans run in parallel
plans.body: Switch between the 1-, 3- and 5-year plans with the tabs. One plan at a time, full width. Each shows its standing, satisfaction, sponsor× modifier and targets with status icons. Click a target to see the board's reaction.

satisfaction.title: Satisfaction drives sponsor and consequences
satisfaction.body: High satisfaction (over 70%) boosts sponsor income. Low satisfaction (under 40%) can trigger salary caps, signing restrictions and ultimately forced rider sales. The progression is gradual and predictable.

kpis.title: What they evaluate on
kpis.body: Results, finances, identity (U25, national core) and ranking, plus newer types: monuments, jerseys, star rider and U25 stat gains. Your club DNA and 5 named board members colour the reactions.

baseline.title: The board is observing your first season
baseline.body: You are in your baseline season. The board is learning your team with no demands or targets. Sponsor modifier stays at 1.0×. When the season ends, negotiations open sequentially: first the 5-year plan, then the 3-year plan, then the 1-year plan.

emptyState.headline: Meet your board
emptyState.intro: You do not have any board plans yet. The board sets targets, evaluates your season and decides how much sponsor income your team gets paid out.

oneYear.label: 1-year plan
oneYear.description: Strict targets, fast results. Full penalty on failure.

threeYear.label: 3-year plan
threeYear.description: Moderate targets, room to grow. 20% reduced penalty.

emptyState.plansFooter: All three plans run in parallel. You negotiate one at a time, and each has its own targets and time horizon.
emptyState.satisfactionFooter: Every met target boosts satisfaction. Dissatisfied boards tighten requirements at the next plan negotiation.

results.label: Results
results.text: stage wins, top placings, total wins

finance.label: Finances
finance.text: outstanding debt, sponsor growth across the plan

identity.label: Identity
identity.text: U25 share, national core, star riders

emptyState.ctaNegotiate: Negotiate your first plan with the board →

planDescriptions.1yr: Strict targets, fast results. Full penalty on failure.
planDescriptions.3yr: Moderate targets, room to grow. 20% reduced penalty.

goal.topNFinishPlanEnd: Top {target} in the division at the end of the plan
goal.signatureRider: {count, plural, one {At least 1 high-renown rider} other {At least # high-renown riders}}
goal.stageWins: {target, plural, one {At least 1 stage win} other {At least # stage wins}}
goal.stageWinsPlanPeriod: At least {target} stage wins over the plan period
goal.gcWins: {target, plural, one {At least 1 overall win} other {At least # overall wins}}
goal.gcWinsPlanPeriod: At least {target} overall wins over the plan period
goal.minU25Riders: Min. {target} U25 riders on the team
goal.sponsorGrowthPlanPeriod: Sponsor income up {target}% over the plan period
goal.noOutstandingDebt: No outstanding debt at season end
goal.monumentPodium: Top 3 in at least {target} Monument {target, plural, one {race} other {races}}
goal.monumentPodiumPlanPeriod: At least {target} podium {target, plural, one {finish} other {finishes}} in Monument races over the plan period
goal.classicsPodium: Top 3 in at least {target} classic {target, plural, one {race} other {races}} (incl. Monuments)
goal.classicsPodiumPlanPeriod: At least {target} podium {target, plural, one {finish} other {finishes}} in classics (incl. Monuments) over the plan period
goal.jerseyWins: At least {target} stage-race {target, plural, one {jersey} other {jerseys}} (points/mountains/young)
goal.jerseyWinsPlanPeriod: At least {target} stage-race jerseys over the plan period
goal.profitableTransfers: Net transfer balance >= {amount} over the plan period
goal.u25DevelopmentDelta: Average U25 stat gain >= {target} stat points/season
goal.relativeRank: Finish ahead of at least {target} other managers in the division
goal.domesticDominance: At least {target} wins in home races per season
goal.tightenedTitle: Tightened by the board after a previous request
goal.relativeRankDetail: You are #{rank} of {total} managers in the division, beating {actual} (target: {target}{check}).
goal.rankInDivisionShort: You are #{rank} of {total} managers in the division.

goalHelp.heading: How is this measured?
goalHelp.signature_rider: Counts riders whose renown is 75 or higher. Renown reflects a rider's fame. An earned reputation system (results, wins, rankings, national honours) is on the way; for now it is a fixed rating.
goalHelp.top_n_finish: Evaluated on your final standing in the last season of the plan, not an average across the plan's seasons.
goalHelp.u25_development_delta: The average stat-point gain per U25 rider per season. Develop young riders so their stats rise over the season.

drivers.heading: What drives this board?
drivers.weightHint: Meeting these goals moves board satisfaction more than the others.

dna.selectIntro: The board has observed season 1 and suggests three directions. DNA shapes target weighting, club tradition targets and which board members you get from the first plan.

rechoose.hint: You can still change your club DNA until your first season ends. After that it's locked in.
rechoose.heading: Change your club DNA
rechoose.intro: Pick a different direction while you're still in your first season. Switching re-assigns your board members and target weighting.

locked.heading: Locked for the season
locked.body: Your club DNA is set for the season and can't be changed. It shapes target weighting, club tradition targets and which board members you get.

skandinavisk_udvikling.label: Scandinavian development team
skandinavisk_udvikling.longDescription: We build riders from the ground up: patience, structure and Nordic work ethic. Sponsors expect development, not fireworks.
skandinavisk_udvikling.traditionGoalLabel: Club DNA: develop the talents, average +3 stat points per season for U25 riders

italiensk_klassiker.label: Italian classics traditionalist
italiensk_klassiker.shortDescription: Spring is sacred. Monuments are the goal
italiensk_klassiker.longDescription: The club is built on the roads of Lombardia and Strade Bianche. Our fans dream of Sanremo and Lombardia, not Tour de France podiums.
italiensk_klassiker.traditionGoalLabel: Club DNA: at least one podium in a classic (incl. Monuments) per plan cycle

sprint_kommerciel.label: Sprint-focused commercial team
sprint_kommerciel.shortDescription: Wins at the line, sponsors on the wheel
sprint_kommerciel.longDescription: Our rider should be first across the line and first in front of the cameras. Sponsor growth comes from visibility, and visibility comes from stage wins.
sprint_kommerciel.traditionGoalLabel: Club DNA: win at least 2 stage-race jerseys per season (sprint focus)

fransk_klatrer.label: French climbing heritage
fransk_klatrer.shortDescription: The Tour mountains are the club's home
fransk_klatrer.longDescription: We have climbed with Anquetil, Hinault and Pinot. The mountains define us, and the Tour de France is still the most important Sunday on the calendar.
fransk_klatrer.traditionGoalLabel: Club DNA: at least 4 French riders in the squad

britisk_allrounder.label: British all-rounder
britisk_allrounder.shortDescription: Discipline across the board, data-led and broad
britisk_allrounder.longDescription: The Sky school lives on. We win through structure, marginal gains and depth, from Roubaix to Andorra. No discipline owns the club, but every discipline belongs here.
britisk_allrounder.traditionGoalLabel: Club DNA: top 3 in the division (broad performance)

suggestionRationale.wildcard: A new path. The board will follow wherever it leads

dna.requiredBeforePlan: Choose your club DNA before negotiating with the board.

identity.wizardTitle: The board's reading of the team
identity.nationalCoreSub: {count, plural, one {1 rider} other {# riders}} · {percent}% of squad
identity.starProfileSub: {count, plural, one {1 star rider} other {# star riders}}
identity.starRidersEmpty: No rider is high-profile enough yet for the board to read as a star.
identity.starRidersExplainer: The board reads these riders as your stars: each one combines high popularity with UCI points above the star threshold. Popularity counts more than results, so a well-known rider can be a star even without big point hauls.
identity.dnaRelation: This reflects your current squad, not your chosen club DNA ({dna}). The board reads the riders you have now; the DNA shapes the long-term goals you negotiate.
identity.dnaRelationNoDna: This reflects your current squad. The board reads the riders you have now.

request.heading: Board request
request.pendingMigration: Board requests are waiting for a database migration
request.errorFallback: Could not send the board request.

layer2.label: Salary cap
layer2.describe: The board has imposed a salary cap of {cash}. You cannot raise the team's total salary, sell a rider first.

layer3.label: Signing restriction
layer3.describe: The board blocks all signings over {cash} until satisfaction recovers.

layer4.label: Forced sale
layer4.describe: The board has force-listed {rider} for {cash}. Contact a buyer to close the deal quickly.

layer5.label: Sponsor pull-out
layer5.describe: A main sponsor has pulled out. Sponsor income is reduced by 10% next season.

bonusOffer.heading: Bonus offer from the board
bonusOffer.body: The board is impressed. They offer <bonus>+{cash}</bonus> to the team's balance for one extra target: <goal>{goal}</goal>.
bonusOffer.footer: Accept and the budget is credited immediately. The extra target is added to your 1-year plan and evaluated at season end.

autoAccept.lastChance: Last chance, the board takes over in {count, plural, one {1 race day} other {# race days}}
autoAccept.waiting: The board is waiting on your negotiation, {count, plural, one {1 race day} other {# race days}} left
autoAccept.footer: If you have not chosen focus and negotiated targets by race day 5, the board picks a plan based on your team identity from season 1.

plan.autoConfigured: Configured automatically at next season start
plan.seasonsRemaining: {count, plural, one {1 season left} other {# seasons left}}
plan.renewLocked: Renegotiation is locked for the rest of the season.

wizard.step1Subtitle: Pick a strategy; the board generates requirements
wizard.horizonDuration: The plan spans {count, plural, one {1 season} other {# seasons}}, targets evaluated continuously
wizard.errorProposal: Could not fetch the board's proposal.
wizard.errorSign: Could not sign the board plan.
wizard.step2Subtitle: Review the board's requirements one at a time
wizard.compromiseHeading: The board has accepted a compromise
wizard.compromiseBody: Penalty halved. Accept the negotiated target?
wizard.negotiationsUsed: {count, plural, one {1 negotiation used} other {# negotiations used}}
wizard.step3Subtitle: {plan}, spans {count, plural, one {1 season} other {# seasons}}
wizard.setupBody: Negotiate your {plan} with the board. Then continue with the next plan.
wizard.setupSequence: The first meeting follows a fixed order: 5-year plan first, then 3-year plan and finally 1-year plan.
wizard.singleRenewalBody: Negotiate a new {plan} with the board.

awaitingData.headline: The board is waiting for more data
awaitingData.summary: The season's signals are still too thin for a clear assessment.

expired.headline: The plan has expired
expired.summary: The board will negotiate a new plan based on the latest evaluation and the team's current direction.

awaitingFirstMarkers.headline: The board is waiting for the season's first markers
awaitingFirstMarkers.summary: {personalitySummary} For now expectations are intact, but the sporting answers only come once the season takes shape.{profileHint}
awaitingFirstMarkers.profileHint: The board reads the team as: {identitySummary}

veryHappy.headline: The board is very satisfied
veryHappy.summary: {strong} is driving the plan forward, and {weakLower} is still under control.

steadyProgress.headline: The board sees steady progress
steadyProgress.summary: {strong} is on track, but {weakLower} needs more focus to keep the plan healthy.

awaitingNext.headline: The board is waiting for the next step
awaitingNext.summary: {weak} is lagging behind the plan, and the pressure rises if the trend does not turn soon.

concerned.headline: The board is concerned
concerned.summary: {weak} is clearly below expectations, and the team lacks visible progress in the current plan.

signalHint.nationalCoreStrong: The {country} core gives the team a clear identity in the board's eyes.
signalHint.nationalCoreWeak: The {country} core still gives the team a clear identity the board will not let go of easily.
signalHint.starHigh: Big-name riders strengthen the sponsor project, but they also keep expectations high.
signalHint.starHighWeak: Big-name riders keep the sponsors interested, but they also push the board to demand more output.
signalHint.starMedium: The team's profiles give the sponsor project a little extra weight.

sponsoraten.label: The Sponsor Director
sponsoraten.shortDescription: Guards sponsor relationships and financial discipline
sponsoraten.longDescription: A former commercial director. Keeps an eye on sponsor deals, the balance sheet and financial risk. Grows nervous around debt and crosses his fingers for sponsor growth every season.

feedback_positive: The sponsors are happy. This holds up.
Now we can finally show the numbers off.
This looks like a well-run project.
Earnings are keeping up with the ambition, perfect.
The board can rest easy now.
feedback_warning: We need to watch the balance from here.
The sponsors are eyeing the numbers nervously.
We should not chase results on borrowed time.
Luck is not a plan, we need stability.
If this turns, it gets expensive.
feedback_negative: This will not hold in the long run.
The sponsors are calling, and not to praise us.
The numbers are saying what we do not want to hear.
We lose trust every time this repeats.
If this continues, there is no budget next year.
goal_proposal: This secures the cash flow.
The sponsor deal will reward this.
Realistic and budget-friendly, yes please.
This gives us room to plan.
Conservative, but that is the strength.
goal_achievement: Exactly what we hoped for, good work.
The sponsors called us themselves to say thanks.
This keeps the budget down to the last detail.
Solid numbers, this is how we build on.
Trust grows with every quarter.
goal_failure: We miss it again, and the sponsors feel it.
This is no longer just an accounting problem.
We are bleeding more than expected.
The investors are asking questions now.
This puts next season's sponsor negotiation at risk.

traditionalisten.label: The Traditionalist
traditionalisten.shortDescription: Weighs the club's heritage and national identity
traditionalisten.longDescription: Sits on the board because he was there in the previous century, or nearly. Wants the club to remind people where it came from. Least enthusiastic about modern flair.

feedback_positive: The club carries its heritage with pride.
This is what the board dreamed of back in the 90s.
The team looks like itself again.
People recognise the club in the peloton.
The soul is intact, that is what matters most.
feedback_warning: We are losing ourselves a little in the chase.
Where has the club's identity gone?
This looks more like other teams than ours.
Tradition is not a bonus box.
Something recognisable is missing.
feedback_negative: We have become a stranger on our own road.
The club is no longer recognisable.
It is like watching another club ride in our jersey.
This is a break with everything the club stands for.
Our fans are weeping, and I nearly am too.
goal_proposal: This looks like the club's DNA.
That is how it has always been with us.
Our fans will feel it.
It is continuity, yes.
The board recognises the club in this.
goal_achievement: True club spirit, this is how it should be.
The forefathers would smile.
This is why we exist.
This is how you build a club that lasts.
The identity stands clearer now.
goal_failure: It is a hard blow to the heritage.
We owe the club an apology.
This would never have happened in the old days.
We are losing ground on the home turf we own.
Where has the pride gone?

talentspejderen.label: The Talent Scout
talentspejderen.longDescription: A former sporting director for a development team. Would rather see a 22-year-old win five years from now than a 32-year-old win today. Knows every U25 stat by heart.

feedback_positive: The talents get room, and they deliver.
This is exactly how you build the future.
In five years everyone will understand this choice.
The youth is blossoming, rarely seen this clearly.
The next generation is stepping up now.
feedback_warning: We must not forget the youth in the chase.
The talents are getting too little racing.
Where is the U25 rotation?
We have better riders in the second team than we are showing.
We are selling the future for a single win.
feedback_negative: We are leaving a whole generation on the bench.
The young riders burn out without racing in their legs.
This is no future for the club.
The talents will flee to other teams next year.
We have misunderstood what a team is.
goal_proposal: This opens doors for the young riders.
Mapped-out development, we will take it.
The young riders need exactly this.
This is how you grow a team organically.
This will lift more than one board metric.
goal_achievement: The talents delivered, just as predicted.
This development creates decades of value.
The U25s have taken the step, wonderful to see.
This is how you send a signal to the scouts.
This confirms the whole development philosophy.
goal_failure: The young riders did not get the chance, our mistake.
We carry the blame for stalled development.
Talent takes longer to recover from this.
We lost something we built over years.
Better rotation would have solved this.

resultatjaegeren.label: The Results Hunter
resultatjaegeren.shortDescription: Wants to win now, everything else is excuses
resultatjaegeren.longDescription: An ex-sporting director with three Tour podiums on his CV. Patience is not a personal strength. Judges a team on wins, podiums and points totals, full stop.

feedback_positive: Wins. Finally, wins.
This is what I was hired to see.
The results speak for themselves.
The rivals are looking nervous now.
This is a winning team, finally.
feedback_warning: The wins are too few and too small.
We are racing top-10 but not top-3.
Taking part is not enough.
The rivals do not feel us yet.
Where is the killer edge that wins races?
feedback_negative: This is unacceptable.
The results are a disaster.
We are invisible in the peloton.
This team should win more, much more.
How many races must we lose before we act?
goal_proposal: Finally, something with teeth.
We win this, or we swap the bikes out.
That is how you set demands.
We will take it. Next target?
The chase is what makes it fun, bring it on.
goal_achievement: The win is in the bag, keep going.
This is the club we know from the good years.
The rivals saw it coming, and could not stop us.
Finally we are delivering, keep going.
This is what the board was elected to see.
goal_failure: This is embarrassing.
We lost to teams we should crush.
Step forward and explain.
That is not a team, it is an excuse.
The next plan sharpens the demands considerably.

pragmatikeren.label: The Pragmatist
pragmatikeren.shortDescription: Seeks the balance between ambition and caution
pragmatikeren.longDescription: A former industry board chair. Looks at everything as a risk and reward ratio. Rarely euphoric, rarely panicked, rarely in agreement with himself two days running.

feedback_positive: This looks like a healthy balance.
Risk and reward are matched well.
The team is delivering on several fronts, good.
It is sustainable, which is what we are after.
No red flags, plenty of green.
feedback_warning: This is on a knife's edge, keep watch.
We are losing a little on every front.
It is balancing too close to the edge.
A little more discipline and it turns.
We are missing one metric that pulls its weight.
feedback_negative: The risk outweighs the reward considerably.
This will not survive another stress test.
We are losing on both sides of the table.
This is not a plan anymore, it is drift.
The board cannot defend this.
goal_proposal: Realistic, we back it.
It matches where we are now.
That is how you avoid overstretching.
It is pragmatic and clear, yes.
This is a good middle path.
goal_achievement: As expected, solid work.
This is what pragmatism is for.
The team delivered without drama.
This is what sustainability looks like.
The next target can be built on top of this.
goal_failure: This should have been within reach.
Disappointing, with no clear excuses.
We will choose the next plan with tighter demands.
This worries me more than it should.
The board expects an open evaluation.

ungdomsidealisten.label: The Youth Idealist
ungdomsidealisten.shortDescription: The club's future is built on young legs
ungdomsidealisten.longDescription: A teacher and former coach in the lower-division system. Wants the club to be known for nurturing talent, without selling it off afterwards. Understands that every winner was young once.

feedback_positive: The young riders get room, that is what it is all about.
The club is a hatchery for the future.
The U25 imprint is clear and beautiful.
Talent grows here, as it should.
This will be remembered fondly.
feedback_warning: We are pushing the young riders into too much racing.
Or, is there even room for U25 at all?
Talent without patience burns out.
We are losing focus on what matters most, the youth.
Where is the long-term line?
feedback_negative: The young riders are suffering, it is heartbreaking to watch.
The club is selling its own future.
This team has forgotten where it came from.
The talents are fleeing to places where they are seen.
We are betraying a whole generation right now.
goal_proposal: This gives the young riders room, perfect.
The board is 100% behind it.
That is how you build the future.
This will bear fruit in years to come.
The U25s will feel seen by this.
goal_achievement: The young riders took responsibility, and delivered.
This is the reward for patience.
We have created an environment they blossom in.
The club can be proud of its development work.
This is what a healthy generation looks like.
goal_failure: We did not give them enough room, regrettably.
This is a hard lesson for the whole system.
We owe the young riders a better structure.
A damaging signal to future talents.
If this continues, the academy will not call.

nationalist_purist.label: The Nationalist Purist
nationalist_purist.shortDescription: The club should represent its home country
nationalist_purist.longDescription: A former national-team coach. Believes a club without a national core is just a sponsor in jerseys. Would rather have one home-grown talent than two foreign stars.

feedback_positive: The home-grown core still shines.
We are the club from our country, no doubt about it.
The home-soil pride is intact.
Our fans hear their own language in the peloton.
This is how a club should sound.
feedback_warning: Our national identity is slipping away.
Fewer home-grown riders, that matters.
We are losing the connection to the fan base.
Home and club should be closely tied.
Where is the next local star?
feedback_negative: The club has become an airport of sponsors.
The national connection is broken.
The fans can no longer see themselves in the team.
This is a commercial transaction, not a club.
We are ashamed of how the roster is put together.
goal_proposal: Home colours, of course.
This affirms the club's roots.
The board stands firmly behind this.
This is how the club stays itself.
It is what the original sponsor would have chosen.
goal_achievement: The home-grown core delivered, fantastic.
This will resonate across the whole country.
This is how you become a national treasure.
Every home win is worth twice as much.
This makes the club irreplaceable.
goal_failure: The home country's gaze weighs heavy now.
This wounds the club at its core.
We have let down more than just the board.
This makes recruitment harder.
We are losing ground we cannot win back easily.

klassiker_purist.label: The Classics Purist
klassiker_purist.shortDescription: Monuments are what make a club immortal
klassiker_purist.longDescription: A former classics rider himself. Believes GC racing is statistics, Roubaix is history. Would rather have one Monument than five stage wins.

feedback_positive: We are heading for a Monument, I can feel it.
The classics spirit hangs in the air.
This is where the club belongs.
Rock-solid roles for the spring campaign.
Rare quality on the roads that count.
feedback_warning: The classics ambition is too soft.
We are missing a hard man for the Roubaix type.
Where is the spring focus?
This team would never win Liège.
That is stage racing, not the heritage.
feedback_negative: We are classics-less, this is embarrassing.
The spring months vanish without a trace.
The team rides past the Monuments without looking up.
This is the wrong club identity for this sport.
We will not be remembered for any of this.
goal_proposal: Monuments, finally on the agenda.
This is what the club was born for.
Spring focus, the board is cheering.
This is how you write history.
We will take it and ride home with a cobblestone.
goal_achievement: A Monument! This is where sport becomes eternal.
This goes in the books forever.
The spring is ours, as it should be.
Cobble by cobble, that is how you build a club.
This is the kind of thing members remember.
goal_failure: The spring campaign flopped, unacceptable.
The Monuments are laughing at us this year.
This rips the heart out of the classics soul.
We need to reinvent the spring plan.
No one takes the club seriously on the Roubaix ramp after this.

gc_elsker.label: The GC Romantic
gc_elsker.shortDescription: Three weeks or nothing, the Tour is everything
gc_elsker.longDescription: A former GC director. The board's biggest dreamer. Wants to build the team up to win a Grand Tour. Does not understand why anyone bothers racing otherwise.

feedback_positive: The GC path is set, we are building towards the Tour.
This is a true grand-tour-rider build.
The mountains are waiting for us.
The three-week form is starting to shine.
We have a team that can win big.
feedback_warning: The GC structure is too soft.
Where are the domestiques for the high mountains?
We lack the third-week resilience.
This team cracks in the Pyrenees.
The Tour dream is on pause.
feedback_negative: We do not have a GC man on the team, unacceptable.
This team would lose 20 minutes on day one.
The Tour is further away than ever.
The board did not get what we were promised.
This is not a serious World Tour project.
goal_proposal: GC focus, finally heading for the big game.
This is where the career is built.
The Tour is the goal, and this is the step.
The board accepts without hesitation.
This is how you raise a captain project.
goal_achievement: The GC result is solid, keep climbing.
This is what roads to the Tour are built on.
The mountains gave respect, good work.
We are heading for the chapter everyone will read.
This is the step before the three weeks.
goal_failure: The GC project is bleeding, this does not help.
We are further from the Tour now than before the season.
This is a regression, not an adjustment.
The mountains crushed us, this must never happen again.
We lack a plan for where the star is going to come from.

lower_results_pressure.label: Lower the results pressure
lower_results_pressure.description: Ask the board for a little more breathing room in the sporting demands of the active plan.
lower_results_pressure.tradeoffPreview: If they say yes, they typically expect tighter financial discipline.

more_youth_focus.label: More youth focus
more_youth_focus.description: Push the plan in a clearer youth direction with more room for development.
more_youth_focus.tradeoffPreview: If they say yes, the U25 identity becomes more central to the active plan.

more_results_focus.label: More results focus now
more_results_focus.description: Ask the board to weight top results higher right away.
more_results_focus.tradeoffPreview: This does not make the plan easier - the sporting demands get sharper.

ease_identity_requirements.label: Ease identity requirements
ease_identity_requirements.description: Ask for a little more flexibility in squad composition and identity goals.
ease_identity_requirements.tradeoffPreview: If they say yes, they typically turn up the sporting pressure instead.

requestReason.planNotActive: Negotiate a new plan before sending requests.
requestReason.alreadyUsed: You have already used this season's board request.
requestReason.windowBlocked: The season's final phase has begun. The board does not take requests in the last {raceDays} race days.
requestReason.midCycleLocked: The {years}-year plan is too early in its cycle to be redirected. The board wants at least {percent}% of the plan completed or a major satisfaction swing before a re-orientation.
requestReason.majorPivotUsed: The board has already accepted a major pivot in this plan cycle. Another big change of direction requires a fresh plan.
requestReason.fallback: The board declines the request right now.

lowerResults.noSportingGoals: The plan has no sporting goals to ease.
lowerResults.tooUnhappy: The board is too dissatisfied to ease the results demands.
lowerResults.needProgress: The board wants to see more progress before easing the results demands.
lowerResults.starPressure: Big profiles keep sponsor expectations high, so the board will not ease the results demands yet.
lowerResults.underEnoughPressure: The board already feels the plan is under enough pressure and wants to see more progress before easing the demands.
lowerResults.starAmbitionLocked: The board refuses to lower the ambition level for a team with clear star profiles, because sponsor expectations are already raised.

moreYouth.alreadyYouth: The plan is already on a youth track.
moreYouth.noIdentityGoal: The plan lacks an identity goal to redirect.
moreYouth.needStability: The board wants to see more stability before switching focus now.
moreYouth.needYouthBase: The board wants to see a clearer youth base in the squad before switching focus.

moreResults.alreadyResults: The plan is already pushing for top results.
moreResults.squadTooThin: The board wants to see a broader squad before raising the ambition level further.

easeIdentity.noIdentityGoal: The plan has no identity requirement to ease.
easeIdentity.needTrust: The board wants more trust before easing the identity requirement.
easeIdentity.needSportingStability: The board wants to see more sporting stability before easing the identity requirement.
easeIdentity.nationalCoreCentral: The board sees the national core as a vital part of the team's DNA and will not let it go yet.
easeIdentity.youthCoreCentral: The board sees the youth track as a core of the team's identity and will not let it go yet.
easeIdentity.needStabilityFirst: The board will not ease the identity requirements until the team stands more stable on the sporting side.
easeIdentity.nationalCoreDna: The board sees the national core as a vital part of the team's DNA and will not let it go yet.

lowerResults.titlePartial: The board gives a little slack
lowerResults.titleTradeoff: The board gives slack at a price
lowerResults.summaryStar: The board only gives a little slack, because a team with big profiles is still held to high expectations.
lowerResults.summaryDefault: The board slightly lowers the sporting pressure in the active plan.
lowerResults.tradeoffStar: The profiles give the sponsor project weight, but they also mean the board tightens the financial demand instead of letting the ambitions go entirely.
lowerResults.tradeoffEconomy: In return the financial demand gets sharper, so the team must be run with more discipline for the rest of the plan.
lowerResults.tradeoffPartial: The board only grants a partial easing, because the plan still needs clear results.

moreYouth.titleBridge: The board only accepts a gradual shift
moreYouth.titleDefault: The board accepts a more youthful track
moreYouth.summaryBridge: The board wants to see more development, but a team with clear star profiles cannot switch tracks all at once.
moreYouth.summaryYouthTrack: The board already sees a youth track in the squad and turns the plan more clearly towards development.
moreYouth.summaryNationalCore: The board sees a clear core in the squad and accepts turning the plan towards a more long-term development direction.
moreYouth.summaryDefault: The plan is turned more towards development and long-term squad building.
moreYouth.tradeoffBridge: The plan becomes more youth-oriented, but the board keeps the focus at a balanced waypoint and does not let go of the results pressure entirely yet.
moreYouth.tradeoffDefault: In return the U25 identity is now a clearer and more lasting requirement in the active plan.

moreResults.titleBridge: The board only accepts a gradual escalation
moreResults.summaryBridge: The board wants to see more results pressure, but a pronounced youth track is only moved gradually towards a more ambitious middle position.
moreResults.summaryStar: Big profiles and sponsor expectations make the board raise the ambition right away.
moreResults.summarySpecialized: The board reads the team as ready to chase bigger results and raises the ambition.
moreResults.summaryDefault: The plan now weights top results even more clearly than before.
moreResults.tradeoffBridge: The board holds on to part of the development track, so the team must prove the new ambition level over time.
moreResults.tradeoffDefault: You get a little more flexibility in the identity requirement, but the results goals have in return become sharper right away.

easeIdentity.titleCore: The board only eases the identity requirement slightly
easeIdentity.titleDefault: The board eases the identity requirement
easeIdentity.summaryCore: The board still sees the national core as a vital part of the team's DNA and only gives a little more flexibility in the identity requirement.
easeIdentity.summaryDefault: The team gets a little more flexibility in squad building and the identity-bearing goals.
easeIdentity.tradeoffCore: The national identity is not let go entirely, and in return the board expects sharper sporting output for the rest of the plan.
easeIdentity.tradeoffDefault: In return the board expects sharper sporting output for the rest of the plan.

errors.requestFallback: Could not send the board request.
